headline: Cash Windfall From Trump’s Tax Law Is Starting to Show Up at Big Companies
summary: The magnitude of the cash savings from this summer’s federal tax legislation is starting to take shape at America’s biggest companies.  AT&T  recently said it expected $1.5 billion to $2 billion in cash tax savings this year, due to provisions in the tax-and-spending law dubbed the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.  The high end of the range is equivalent to an 11% boost to analyst estimates of 2025 free cash flow before the law was enacted.

headline: Thai Data Center Capacity May Triple on Surging Demand for AI
summary: The nation’s capacity is expected to rise to about 1 gigawatts in 2027 from 350 megawatts in 2024, said Supparat Singhara Na Ayutthaya, vice chairman of the Thai Data Center Association.  A data center requires an investment of about $10 million for each megawatt, he said.  “Thailand has drawn tremendous interest for new data center investments with its steady power generation and water supply,” Supparat said in an interview this week.

headline: Volkswagen extends Amazon cloud partnership to save costs with AI
summary: BERLIN (Reuters) -Volkswagen has extended its factory cloud partnership with Amazon Web Services by another five years, the German carmaker said on Thursday, as it rolls out the use of artificial intelligence to improve production and reduce costs.  The company partnered with the cloud computing services unit of Amazon.com to create a digital production platform, or DPP, which Volkswagen says is already used in 43 locations in Europe, North America and South America.  Globally, Volkswagen has over 114 production sites.
